site.tagline: Local food, deli and café in the former NatWest bank in Ellesmere
site.metaDescription: Moolah is a local food store, deli and café on Scotland Street in Ellesmere, with a licensed café bar, snug seating, local produce and plenty of reasons to stay for coffee, brunch or lunch.
site.heroTitle: Local food, deli finds and café comfort in the heart of Ellesmere.
site.heroIntro: Moolah brings together a local food shop, deli shelves, fresh produce and a relaxed café atmosphere inside the former NatWest bank on Scotland Street. It is a place to browse, meet, eat, drink and take a few good things home.
site.aboutTitle: A food shop and café with real local character.
site.aboutIntro: Public descriptions of Moolah consistently point to local produce, a licensed café bar, the converted former bank building and the snug-like seating areas that make it feel different from a standard coffee stop.
site.aboutStory: The website is written to reflect what visitors already respond to: coffee and cake, brunch and lunch, local food and drink, deli browsing, friendly service and a welcoming atmosphere that feels rooted in Ellesmere.
site.deliIntro: The deli side deserves equal space online. Moolah is known for local produce, cheeses, bread, biscuits, pies, vegetables, drinks and take-home treats as well as the café offer.
site.visitIntro: Find Moolah on Scotland Street in Ellesmere, inside the former NatWest bank building. Use the site for opening hours, current specials, menu highlights and simple contact details.
site.bookingNote: For bookings or current availability, please call the shop directly or message via Facebook.

specials.title: Local produce on the shelves
specials.text: Start with a strong first impression: local food, deli finds, fresh fruit and veg, drinks and take-home treats.

specials.title: Coffee, cake and the snug
specials.text: Use the homepage to sell the atmosphere as much as the menu, especially the cosy snug and lounge-style seating.

specials.title: Brunch, lunch and light bites
specials.text: Point visitors straight to current menu highlights, homemade options and easy café favourites.

specials.title: Licensed café-bar feel
specials.text: Give the site a slightly more grown-up feel by showing the beer, wine and local drinks side as well.

menuSections.title: Breakfast, brunch and lunch
menuSections.intro: Public listings and social snippets highlight breakfast, brunch, lunch and daytime café dining.

menuSections.title: Cakes, coffee and hot drinks
menuSections.intro: Reviews repeatedly mention coffee, loose leaf tea, cake and a welcoming stop-in atmosphere.

menuSections.title: Pies, deli favourites and savouries
menuSections.intro: Public comments mention veggie pies, bean and cheese pies, hummus, salads and other café staples.

menuSections.title: Shop and drinks range
menuSections.intro: The shop side is regularly described as a reason to browse, with local food and drink well beyond a standard café counter.

deliSections.title: Local cheeses and deli browsing
deliSections.text: Show cheeses, savouries and take-home food as part of the main journey, not tucked away at the bottom of the page.

deliSections.title: Fresh produce and pantry staples
deliSections.text: Fresh fruit and veg, bread, biscuits and useful everyday food lines help Moolah feel like a destination as well as a café.

deliSections.title: Licensed drinks and giftable finds
deliSections.text: Public descriptions mention beer, wine and local drinks. That gives the shop side a stronger premium feel.
